mnemonics.title: Web Security Threat Categories
mnemonics.content:
**I**ntegrity **C**onfidentiality **D**oS **A**uthentication = **ICDA**
I = data modification in transit
C = eavesdropping on web traffic
D = overwhelming the server
A = impersonating users or servers

mnemonics.title: Three Attack Locations
mnemonics.content:
**S**erver **C**lient **N**etwork = **SCN**
Server: vulnerabilities, misconfiguration
Client: malicious scripts, plugins, cookies
Network: eavesdropping, MITM, replay
All three must be secured for web safety

mnemonics.title: Web Injection Attacks
mnemonics.content:
**X**SS = scripts in **X**tra pages (cross-site)
**SQL**i = **S**neaky **Q**uery **L**anguage injection
**CSRF** = **C**ookie-riding **S**neaky **R**equest **F**orgery
Defense: validate inputs, sanitize, parameterize

mnemonics.title: Cookie Security Flags
mnemonics.content:
**S**ecure **H**ttpOnly **S**ameSite = **SHS**
Secure: HTTPS only (no plaintext)
HttpOnly: no JavaScript access (anti-XSS)
SameSite: no cross-site sending (anti-CSRF)
All three flags should be set on sensitive cookies

mnemonics.title: Security at Network Layers
mnemonics.content:
**N**etwork = **IP**Sec (transparent, all traffic)
**T**ransport = **TLS** (HTTPS, most common)
**A**pplication = **PGP**, **S/MIME**, Kerberos
Remember: NTA - Network, Transport, Application
TLS is the most widely used for web security

keyPoints: Web security threats target integrity, confidentiality, availability (DoS), and authentication.
Threats can occur at the server, client (browser), or network (traffic in transit).
Common web attacks: XSS, SQL injection, CSRF, session hijacking, DoS/DDoS.
HTTPS (HTTP over TLS/SSL) provides encryption, integrity, and server authentication.
Security can be applied at network (IPSec), transport (TLS), or application (PGP, S/MIME) layers.
commonMistakes: Assuming HTTPS alone makes a website fully secure - it only protects data in transit, not against XSS, SQL injection, or server-side vulnerabilities.
Confusing XSS and SQL injection: XSS injects scripts that run in the USER's browser; SQL injection targets the SERVER's database.
Thinking client-side validation alone is sufficient - attackers can bypass client-side checks, so server-side validation is essential.
